headline: Software and Healthcare Lead as Semis Crater to Open Q3
body_html:
Stocks are navigating a fractured session at the mid-point of the day, with the broad tape essentially split down the middle — 99 names advancing against 98 declining across the 198-stock universe — as the third quarter opens on an ambiguous note. The dominant theme is a sharp rotation away from chip-related names and toward software and healthcare, a reversal that has last year's laggards commanding the headlines. Federal Reserve Governor Warsh's comments that inflation risks have eased provided enough of a tailwind to lift the S&P 500 and the Dow into positive territory, even as factory data showed U.S. manufacturing activity cooling from a four-year high while input prices remained stubbornly elevated.

Software is the clear sector leader, up 1.7%, with cybersecurity and database names pacing the group. Healthcare follows at plus 1.19%, buoyed by a standout move in diagnostics. Consumer staples are modestly positive. On the other side of the ledger, semiconductors are the session's biggest casualty, off 4.13%, with equipment makers bearing the brunt of the selling; Homebuilders and Materials are also under meaningful pressure.

Into the close, traders will be watching Iran-related headlines carefully — conflicting signals around Hormuz control, U.S.-Iran technical talks, and regional tensions are injecting geopolitical noise that could shift energy and risk sentiment quickly. The semiconductor tape and any follow-through in software momentum are the two technical stories worth tracking through the final hour.

briefs.headline: Guardant Health Surges to Lead All Gainers Mid-Session
briefs.body: Shares of Guardant Health, the liquid-biopsy diagnostics company, are up nearly 14% intraday, making it the standout mover across the entire universe on Tuesday. The move places healthcare diagnostics squarely at the center of today's rotation story, with the broader Healthcare sector adding 1.19% and outpacing most of the tape.

briefs.headline: Semiconductor Equipment Names Take a Beating Across the Board
briefs.body: KLA Corp, Lam Research, and Applied Materials are each off between roughly 10% and 12% mid-session, dragging the Semiconductors sector down 4.13% — the worst performer in today's universe by a wide margin. Teradyne and Sandisk are also shedding more than 10%, pointing to broad-based pressure rather than a single company-specific event.

briefs.headline: CrowdStrike Climbs Nearly 10% After Bullish Analyst Note
briefs.body: CrowdStrike is rallying close to 10% intraday, emerging as one of the session's top performers after what market commentators are characterizing as an unusually constructive analyst call on a software name. The move reinforces the session's software leadership theme, with the sector posting the best gains in the universe at plus 1.7%.

briefs.headline: Corning Slides 13% to Sit at the Bottom of the Decline List
briefs.body: Corning is the session's worst performer, shedding nearly 13% and dragging the Materials-Glass segment lower. The drop contributes to a rough day for the broader Materials sector, which is off 2.71%, as investors appear to be shedding cyclical exposure on the first trading day of the third quarter.

briefs.headline: Iran Signals Complicate Energy and Risk Markets at Midday
briefs.body: A stream of contradictory headlines — U.S.-Iran technical talks aimed at a peace framework, Tehran's insistence on retaining influence over the Strait of Hormuz, and Iranian warnings against any perceived threat — is injecting geopolitical uncertainty into midday trading. Energy is off 0.74%, a relatively contained loss given the noise, though traders are watching developments closely.

briefs.headline: U.S. Manufacturing Cools From Peak but Price Pressures Linger
briefs.body: Reuters data showed American factory activity easing back from a four-year high in the latest read, offering a mixed signal for the industrial economy as Q3 begins. Critically, input prices remained elevated, a detail that complicates the Federal Reserve's path even as Governor Warsh suggested inflation risks have moderated — a comment that helped push major indices into positive territory.
